title: cynoglossum cheirifolium var. lanatum
intro: Cynoglossum cheirifolium var. lanatum, also known as hairy tongue flower or hairy lipper, is a species of flowering plant in the borage family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic, to treat various health issues such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting digestion, due to its high content of saponins and other bioactive compounds. Herbal preparations of Cynoglossum cheirifolium var. lanatum can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used in traditional medicine to support urinary health and alleviate symptoms associated with kidney stones.
